subject: Product Management
title: Problem vs Solution
description: A quick win: understand Problem vs Solution.

hook.text: Most product teams start with a solution: 'Let's build a chatbot.' Great teams start with a problem: 'Users can't find answers.'

buildup.text: Falling in love with a solution is the #1 cause of wasted product effort. The solution anchors all thinking.

discovery.text: Staying in the problem space longer opens up multiple possible solutions — some far better than the first idea.

twist.text: Einstein supposedly said: 'If I had an hour to solve a problem, I'd spend 55 minutes understanding it.'

climax.text: The discipline to resist jumping to solutions is what separates discovery-driven teams from feature factories.

punchline.text: Fall in love with the problem, not the solution.

quiz.question: Why should product teams focus on problems before solutions?
options: Staying in the problem space reveals multiple better solutions
Problems are easier to discuss than solutions
Solutions don't matter in product management
